senses.definition: a condition where the eyelids close in fast, involuntary rhythmic movements
examples: The neurologist diagnosed blepharoclonus after noticing rhythmic eyelid closures.
Blepharoclonus can be linked to problems with the brain’s control of movement.
His symptoms included frequent, involuntary blinking that looked rhythmic.

senses.definition: a congenital defect of the eyelid in which part of the lid is missing or formed abnormally
examples: She was born with blepharocoloboma affecting the shape of her eyelid.
Blepharocoloboma is considered a structural eye-lid abnormality present from birth.
The doctor explained that blepharocoloboma can change how the eye closes.

senses.definition: inflammation affecting both the eyelid margin and the conjunctiva (the eye’s inner surface)
examples: He developed blepharoconjunctivitis and complained of burning and gritty eyes.
Blepharoconjunctivitis often starts with eyelid irritation and then involves the conjunctiva.
The clinic treated blepharoconjunctivitis with medication for both eyelids and eye surface.

senses.definition: a condition involving separation or abnormal widening of the eyelid tissues
examples: The specialist mentioned blepharodiastasis when describing the eyelid’s abnormal spacing.
Blepharodiastasis may contribute to problems with eyelid closure.
Imaging helped confirm blepharodiastasis in her case.

senses.definition: an eyelid color or pigmentation abnormality
examples: The report listed blepharodyschroia as the reason for the unusual eyelid color.
Blepharodyschroia can make the eyelid look darker or differently colored.
A clinician examined her for blepharodyschroia during the eye exam.

senses.definition: a rare condition where blood-tinged sweat or fluid comes from the eyelids or around the eyes
examples: Doctors suspected blepharohematidrosis after observing blood-tinged discharge near the eyelid.
Blepharohematidrosis is unusual and needs medical evaluation.
Her symptoms were rare episodes of reddish fluid around the eye area.

senses.definition: the presence of small stones or stone-like deposits in the eyelids
examples: The ophthalmologist found blepharolithiasis during the exam.
Blepharolithiasis can cause irritation and discomfort along the eyelid.
Treatment focused on removing the deposits and calming inflammation.

senses.definition: darkening or discoloration of the eyelid skin
examples: Her dermatologist diagnosed blepharomelasma after noticing dark patches on her eyelids.
Blepharomelasma can be similar to other hyperpigmentation conditions.
The eye-area pigmentation looked uneven, consistent with blepharomelasma.

senses.definition: a condition involving a growth or tumor-like change of the eyelid
examples: The doctor used the term blepharoncosis to describe the abnormal eyelid growth.
Blepharoncosis needs proper eye examination and diagnosis.
She was referred to an eye specialist after noticing a persistent eyelid lump.

senses.definition: an eyelid growth or lesion, often referring to a tumor-like change
examples: They documented a blepharoncus after evaluating his eyelid lesion.
A blepharoncus can cause swelling and irritation.
Because the bump did not go away, the patient sought ophthalmic care.

senses.definition: a condition where the opening of the eyelids becomes abnormally narrow
examples: Blepharophimosis can make the eyes look less open than usual.
The eye doctor measured the eyelid opening to assess blepharophimosis.
In her childhood history, she was described as having blepharophimosis.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure that corrects abnormal eyelid features (often involving the eyebrow/upper eyelid area)
examples: She underwent blepharophryplasty to correct her eyelid abnormality.
Blepharophryplasty is done by an ophthalmic or plastic surgeon.
The procedure aims to improve eyelid shape and function.

senses.definition: a disease or disorder affecting the eyelids and the surface of the eye
examples: The term blepharophthalmia appeared in the medical notes describing his eye problem.
Blepharophthalmia can involve irritation around the eyelids and eye surface.
A clinician used the term to describe combined eyelid and eye inflammation.

senses.definition: a thickening or swelling of the eyelid skin (a wart-like or enlarged eyelid change)
examples: Her eyelid skin looked thickened, consistent with blepharophyma.
Blepharophyma can cause cosmetic changes and irritation.
The specialist examined the eyelid to confirm blepharophyma.

senses.definition: a device or method related to reshaping or reconstructing the eyelid
examples: The surgeon used a blepharoplast technique as part of eyelid reconstruction.
The term blepharoplast appears in discussions of eyelid repair.
Her care plan included blepharoplast as a reconstructive option.

senses.definition: relating to surgery or procedures that correct or improve the eyelids
examples: She chose a blepharoplastic procedure to improve the appearance of her eyelids.
The doctor explained the blepharoplastic options available for her condition.
Blepharoplastic surgery focuses on eyelid structure and function.

senses.definition: surgical operations that reshape or correct the eyelids
examples: He had blepharoplasties to address both upper and lower eyelids.
Blepharoplasties can be cosmetic or medically necessary.
The surgeon discussed recovery after multiple blepharoplasties.

senses.definition: plural of blepharoplast, referring to procedures or devices for eyelid reshaping or reconstruction
examples: The clinic offers several blepharoplasts as options for eyelid repair.
Some blepharoplasts are designed for reconstructive work.
He learned about different blepharoplasts before choosing treatment.

senses.definition: surgery to improve or reshape the eyelids
examples: She scheduled a blepharoplasty to remove extra skin around her eyelids.
Blepharoplasty can help with a tired look and sometimes with eyelid function.
The surgeon explained the risks and recovery for blepharoplasty.

senses.definition: paralysis or loss of movement in the eyelids
examples: He was diagnosed with blepharoplegia after his eyelids stopped moving normally.
Blepharoplegia can cause trouble closing the eyes.
The neurologist checked eyelid strength as part of blepharoplegia assessment.

senses.definition: drooping of the eyelid, especially the upper eyelid
examples: She noticed her upper eyelid drooping and was diagnosed with blepharoptosis.
Blepharoptosis can be present at birth or develop later.
The doctor discussed treatment options for blepharoptosis.

senses.definition: pus or discharge from the eyelids, often from infection or inflamed eyelid glands
examples: The doctor described blepharopyorrhea because there was discharge along the eyelid.
Blepharopyorrhea may come with redness and irritation.
Treatment was started to control the infection causing blepharopyorrhea.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure that stitches or tightens the eyelid to protect the eye or correct an eyelid problem
examples: Blepharorrhaphy was recommended to help protect his eye after a severe eyelid problem.
The surgeon performed blepharorrhaphy to reduce eye exposure.
After blepharorrhaphy, the patient was asked to follow careful wound care.

senses.definition: involuntary tightening or spasms of the eyelid muscles that can cause the eye to close
examples: She developed blepharospasm, and her eyes sometimes closed for several seconds.
Blepharospasm can be triggered by bright light or stress.
The neurologist treated her blepharospasm to reduce the force and frequency of spasms.

senses.definition: spasms of the eyelid muscles that cause involuntary eye closure
examples: Her blepharospasms got worse when she was tired.
Doctors observed frequent blepharospasms during the exam.
The medication reduced the number of blepharospasms each day.
